Royalty splits generally follow standard industry agreements; however, the arrangement differs based on whether the publishing rights are owned by the company or retained by the artist.\n​​Additionally, for some songs, company owns only the master rights and not the publishing rights, while for certain songs they hold complete rights. Therefore, the structure and economics vary on a case-to-case basis.\nHe stated that publishing is generally a part of each music label, which includes servicing brands that approach them for music-related requirements.
